oids.name: zxAnGponOPName
oids.description: Name of ONU profile.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPMappingTable
oids.description: The ONU profile mapping table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPMappingEntry
oids.description: The ONU profile mapping entry.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPMappingName
oids.description: Name of service.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPMappingIfId
oids.description:
4bytes value contains:type (MSB 2bytes)/ifId (LSB 2bytes).
             type   means
             0      bridge
             1      ethuni
             2      iphost
             3      wifiuni
             4      veip
             5      wan

             If type is 'bridge', then the 2-byte ifId has no meaning, just keep
             it zero;
             If type is 'ethuni', then the 2-byte ifId contains:
             slot (MSB 1byte)/port (LSB 1byte);
             If type is 'iphost', then the 2-byte ifId means the ID of iphost,
             range from 1 to 255;
             If type is 'wifiuni', then the 2-byte ifId contains:
             slot (MSB 1byte)/port (LSB 1byte);
             If type is 'veip', then the 2-byte ifId means the ID of VEIP,
             range from 1 to 255;
             If type is 'wan', then the 2-byte ifId means the ID of WAN,
             range from 1 to 255.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPMappingType
oids.description:
Map type of service.
             - cos: mapping to GEM port by CoS value
             - vlan: mapping to GEM port by VLAN value
             - cosAndVlan: mapping to GEM port by both cos and VLAN value
             - transparent: mapping to GEM port transparently
             - untag: mapping to GEMP port for untag frames

oids.name: zxAnGponOPMappingCos
oids.description:
A bitfield defines the frames with the priority related with this
             service. MSB stands for priority 7, LSB stands for priority 0.
             A bit value of 0 specifies that frames with the associated priority
             are to be discarded.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPMappingVid
oids.description:
Each 2 bytes stand for the VLAN ID related with this service.
             The first N*2 bytes are valid, until VLAN ID=0.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPMappingRowStatus
oids.description:
The status of this conceptual row.
             The state of this conceptual row only support
             'not exist' and  'active'.
             The action of this conceptual row only support
             'createAndGo' and 'destroy'.

             To create a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to createAndGo(4), and must comply
             with the multi-variables usage rules in 'zxAnGponOPMappingEntry'
             description.

             To delete a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to destroy(6).

             To query this object, always return 1.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBpConfTable
oids.description: The ONU profile VPort table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBpConfEntry
oids.description: The ONU profile VPort entry.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBpVportId
oids.description: VPort ID.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBpName
oids.description: Name of the VPort.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBpMapType
oids.description:
Map type of GEM port and VPort.
             cos(1): mapping by CoS value.
             vlan(2): mapping by VLAN value.
             cosPlusVlan(3): mapping by CoS and VLAN value.
             oneToOne(4): mapping is 1:1 between GEM port and VPort.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBpRowStatus
oids.description:
The status of this conceptual row.

             The supported actions of this conceptual row only include:

             To create a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to createAndGo(4).

             To delete a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to destroy(6).
            
             To query this object, always return 1.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBpMapConfTable
oids.description: The ONU profile VPort mapping table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBpMapConfEntry
oids.description: The ONU profile VPort mapping entry.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBpMapConfCos
oids.description: A CoS value (0..7) referenced by a GEM port mapped to a VPort.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBpMapConfVid
oids.description:
A VLAN ID referenced by a GEM port mapped to a VPort. 
             The value 65535 indicates the untagged frames.
             When VPort mapping type is oneToOne(4) or cos(1), this value is 0.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBpMapConfRowStatus
oids.description:
The status of this conceptual row.
            
             The supported actions of this conceptual row only include:

             To create a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to createAndGo(4).

             To delete a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to destroy(6).
             
             To query this object, always return 1.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVlanPortTable
oids.description:
The ONU profile port VLAN table.
             A table containing VLAN port information for VLANs
             configured into the bridge by (local or network)
             management,  specifying the set of
             ports to which frames received on a VLAN for this FDB
             and containing a specific Group destination address are
             allowed to be forwarded.

             When a manager wants to create a VLAN port,it must comply
             with the multi-variables usage rule that all the mib variables
             related to same fuction must be in one snmp set operation, the
             variables have two type: mandatory and optional.

             Mandatory variables must be in the one snmp set operation.
             Optional variables may be in the one snmp set operation with the
             mandatory variables, but must not be used alone.

             To modify a service port,  there are several cases base on the
             variable zxAnGponOPVlanPortMode, and the mandatory and optional
             variables for each case are different.

             Case 1: to modify a VLAN port when zxAnGponOPVlanPortMode is
             access(1):
             - mandatoty:
             zxAnGponOPVlanPortMode,
             - optional :
             zxAnGponOPVlanPortDefaultVid,
             zxAnGponOPVlanPortDefaultCos,

             Case 2: to modify a service port when zxAnGponOPVlanPortMode is
             trunk(2):
             - mandatoty:
             zxAnGponOPVlanPortMode,
             - optional :

             Case 3: to modify a service port when zxAnGponOPVlanPortMode is
             hybrid(3):
             - mandatoty:
             zxAnGponOPVlanPortMode,
             - optional :
             zxAnGponOPVlanPortDefaultVid,
             zxAnGponOPVlanPortDefaultCos,

             Case 4: to modify a service port when zxAnGponOPVlanPortMode is
             transparent(4):
             - mandatoty:
             zxAnGponOPVlanPortMode,
             - optional :

             Case 5: to modify a service port when zxAnGponOPVlanPortMode is
             none(5):
             - mandatoty:
             zxAnGponOPVlanPortMode,
             - optional :

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVlanPortEntry
oids.description:
The ONU profile port VLAN entry.
             Supporting Ethernet UNI, WiFi UNI, VEIP, GEM port, 
             multicast GEM port, WAN and xDSL UNI.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVlanPortMode
oids.description:
The access mode of the port. There are five types:
             - access: access mode of port access
             - trunk: trunk mode of port access
             - hybrid: hybrid mode of port access
             - transparent: transparent mode of port access
             - none: delete all the VLAN configuration of the port,
                  restore to ONU's internal policy.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVlanPortDefaultVid
oids.description: The PVID of the port.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVlanPortDefaultCos
oids.description: The default priority of the port.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVlanPortVlanList
oids.description: The VLAN sequence of the port.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVlanPortCmdTable
oids.description:
The port VLAN operation command table.
             This table is used for ONU profile.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVlanPortCmdEntry
oids.description:
The port VLAN operation command entry.
             Supporting Ethernet UNI, WiFi UNI, VEIP, GEM port, 
             multicast GEM port, WAN and xDSL UNI.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVlanPortCmd
oids.description:
The command to operate VLAN list of 'zxAnGponOPVlanPortTable'.

             The multi-variables binding rule should be complied with
             when a manager configures port VLAN cmd. According to this rule,
             all the related mib variables must be included in one SNMP
             set operation.

             There are two types of variables: mandatory and optional.
             All of the mandatory variables must be included in one SNMP
             set operation. Optional variables may be included in one
             SNMP set operation with the mandatory variables, but must
             not be used alone.

             To configure VLAN, the mandatory and optional variables
             are as follows:
             mandatoty: zxAnGponOPVlanPortCmd, zxAnGponOPVlanPortVid
             optional : none

             There are two groups of operation command, the first group includes
             addVlan and delVlan, the second group includes addAllVlan and 
             delAllVlan. For an ONU interface, two groups of operation command 
             can't be use at the same time. When zxAnGponOPVlanPortCmd is 
             addAllVlan or delAllVlan and then zxAnGponOPVlanPortVid is 
             invalid.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVlanPortVid
oids.description:
The port VLAN ID.
             This variable must comply with the multi-variables binding
             rule described in 'zxAnGponOPVlanPortCmd'.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVlanTransTable
oids.description: The ONU profile port VLAN translation table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVlanTransEntry
oids.description:
The ONU profile port VLAN translation entry.
             Supporting Ethernet UNI, WiFi UNI, VEIP, GEM port, 
             multicast GEM port, WAN and xDSL UNI.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVlanTransUserVid
oids.description: User VLAN ID for translation.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVlanTransUserCos
oids.description:
User priority for translation, the range of priority is from 0
             to 8, and '8' indicates no configuration.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVlanTransCVid
oids.description:
Inner VLAN for VLAN translate, '0' indicates no translation.
             The multi-variables binding rule should be complied with
             when a manager configures port VLAN translate. According to this
             rule, all the related mib variables must be included in one SNMP
             set operation.

             There are two types of variables: mandatory and optional.
             All of the mandatory variables must be included in one SNMP
             set operation. Optional variables may be included in one
             SNMP set operation with the mandatory variables, but must
             not be used alone.

             To configure VLAN translation, the mandatory and optional variables
             are as follows:
             mandatoty: zxAnGponOPVlanTransSVid
             optional : zxAnGponOPVlanTransCVid, zxAnGponOPVlanTransCtagCos,
                       zxAnGponOPVlanTransStagCos

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVlanTransCtagCos
oids.description:
Inner VLAN priority for VLAN translate, '8' indicates no
             translation.
             This variable must comply with the multi-variables binding
             rule described in 'zxAnGponOPVlanTransCVid'.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVlanTransSVid
oids.description:
Outer VLAN for VLAN translate, '0' indicates no SVLAN.
             This variable must comply with the multi-variables binding
             rule described in 'zxAnGponOPVlanTransCVid'.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVlanTransStagCos
oids.description:
Outer VLAN priority for VLAN translate, '8' indicates copy priority
             from inner VLAN priority.
             This variable must comply with the multi-variables binding
             rule described in 'zxAnGponOPVlanTransCVid'.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVlanTransRowStatus
oids.description:
The status of this conceptual row.

             To create a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to createAndGo(4).

             In particular, a newly created row cannot be created
             until the corresponding at least one of
             'zxAnGponOPVlanTransCVid
              zxAnGponOPVlanTransCtagCos
              zxAnGponOPVlanTransSVid
              zxAnGponOPVlanTransStagCos'
             have been set.

             To delete a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to destroy(6).
             To query this object, always return 1.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPMVlanTable
oids.description: The ONU profile multicast VLAN table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPMVlanEntry
oids.description: The ONU profile multicast VLAN entry.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPMVid
oids.description: This attribute represents the VLAN ID (1~4094).

oids.name: zxAnGponOPMVlanRowStatus
oids.description:
The status of this conceptual row.
             The state of this conceptual row only support
             'not exist' and  'active'.
             The action of this conceptual row only support
             'createAndGo' and 'destroy'.

             To create a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to createAndGo(4).

             To delete a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to destroy(6).

             To query this object, always return 1.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPIgmpConfTable
oids.description: The ONU profile IGMP configuration table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPIgmpConfEntry
oids.description:
The ONU profile IGMP configuration entry.
             Supporting Ethernet UNI and VEIP.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPIgmpUsedPrfName
oids.description:
This attribute points to an instance of the multicast operations
             profile.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPIgmpMaxGroups
oids.description:
This attribute specifies the maximum number of dynamic multicast
             groups that may be replicated to the client port at any one time.
             The default value 0 specifies that no administrative limit is to
             be imposed.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPIgmpMaxBw
oids.description:
This attribute specifies the maximum imputed dynamic bandwidth,
             in kbps, that may be delivered to the client port at
             any one time.
             The default value 0 specifies that no administrative limit is to
             be imposed.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPIgmpBwEnforce
oids.description:
enabled: attempts to exceed the max multicast bandwidth
             be counted and denied.
             disabled: such attempts be counted but honored.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPIgmpConfRowStatus
oids.description:
The status of this conceptual row.
             The state of this conceptual row only support
             'not exist' and  'active'.
             The action of this conceptual row only support
             'createAndGo' and 'destroy'.

             To create a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to createAndGo(4).

             To delete a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to destroy(6).

             To query this object, always return 1.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTrafficSchedTable
oids.description: The ONU profile traffic scheduler configuration table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTrafficSchedEntry
oids.description:
The ONU profile traffic scheduler configuration entry.
             Supporting Ethernet UNI.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTrafficSchedPrfName
oids.description:
The ONU UNI traffic scheduler profile name.
             The empty string indicates no profile is bounded.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPExtVlanTable
oids.description: The ONU profile extended VLAN configuration table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPExtVlanEntry
oids.description: The ONU profile extended VLAN configuration entry.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPExtVlanInputTpid
oids.description:
This attribute gives the special TPID value for operations
             on the input (filtering) side of the table. Typical values
             include 0x8a88 and 0x9100. Default value is 0x8100.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPExtVlanOutputTpid
oids.description:
This attribute gives the special TPID value for operations
             on the output (tagging) side of the table. Typical values
             include 0x8a88 and 0x9100. Default value is 0x8100.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPExtVlanDsMode
oids.description:
Regardless of its association, the extended VLAN tagging
             operation configuration data ME pertains to upstream traffic.
             This attribute specifies the mode for downstream mapping.
             1 The operation performed in the downstream direction is the
                inverse of that performed in the upstream direction.
                For one-to-one VLAN mappings, the inverse is trivially defined.
                Many-to-one mappings are possible, however, and these are
                treated as follows. If the many-to-one mapping results from
                multiple operation rules producing the same ANI-side tag
                configuration, then the first rule in the list defines the
                inverse operation. If the many-to-one mapping results from
                don't care fields in the filter being replaced with provisioned
                fields in the ANI-side tags, then the inverse is defined to
                set the corresponding fields on the ANI side with their
                lowest value.
             2 No operation is performed in the downstream direction.
             3 Filter on VID and p-bit value. On a match, perform the inverse 
                operation on both the VID and p-bit value. If no match is found,
                forward the frame unmodified.
             4 Filter on VID only. On a match, perform the inverse VID operation
                only; pass the p bits through. If no match is found, forward the
                frame unmodified.
             5 Filter on p-bit only. On a match, perform the inverse p-bit
                operation only; pass the VID through. If no match is found,
                forward the frame unmodified.
             6 Filter on VID and p-bit value. On a match, perform the inverse
                operation on both the VID and p-bit value. If no match is found,
                discard the frame.
             7 Filter on VID. On a match, perform the inverse operation on the
                VID only; pass the p bits through. If no match is found, discard
                the frame.
             8 Filter on p-bit only. On a match, perform the inverse p-bit
                operation only; pass the VID through. If no match is found,
                discard the frame.
             9 Regardless of the filter rules, discard all downstream traffic.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPExtVlanDscp2CosPrf
oids.description:
This attribute refers to 'zxAnGponRmDscp2CosPrfName', specifies
             mapping from DSCP to P-bits. The attribute can be considered a
             bit string sequence of 64 3-bit groups. The 64 sequence entries
             represent the possible values of the 6-bit DSCP field. Each
             3-bit group specifies the P-bit value to which the associated
             DSCP value should be mapped.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPExtVlanRowStatus
oids.description:
The status of this conceptual row.
             The state of this conceptual row only support
             'not exist' and  'active'.
             The action of this conceptual row only support
             'createAndGo' and 'destroy'.

             To create a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to createAndGo(4).

             To delete a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to destroy(6).

             To query this object, always return 1.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPExtVlanRuleConfTable
oids.description: The ONU profile extended VLAN rule configuration table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPExtVlanRuleConfEntry
oids.description: The ONU profile extended VLAN rule configuration entry.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPExtVlanConfFiltRule
oids.description:
This attribute refers to 'zxAnGponRmFiltRuleName'
             provides a unique profile of filter for this extended VLAN table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPExtVlanConfTreatRule
oids.description:
This attribute refers to 'zxAnGponRmTrtRuleName'
             provides a unique profile of treatment for this extended VLAN
             table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPExtVlanConfRowStatus
oids.description:
The status of this conceptual row.
             The state of this conceptual row only support
             'not exist' and  'active'.
             The action of this conceptual row only support
             'createAndGo' and 'destroy'.

             To create a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to createAndGo(4).

             To delete a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to destroy(6).

             To query this object, always return 1.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBrgConfTable
oids.description: The ONU profile bridge configuration table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBrgConfEntry
oids.description: The ONU profile bridge configuration entry.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBrgIndex
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the index of MAC bridge in ONU, from 1 to
             65535. The first byte specifies the slot ID associated with this 
             MAC bridge, the second byte specifies the bridge group ID 
             associated with this MAC bridge.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBrgStpEnable
oids.description:
This attribute indicates whether or not a spanning tree
             algorithm is enabled.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBrgLearningEnable
oids.description:
This attribute indicates whether or not the learning
             function of the bridge is enabled.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBrgLocalBridgeEnable
oids.description:
This attribute indicates whether or not the bridging between UNI
             ports is enabled.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBrgPri
oids.description:
This attribute specifies the bridge priority in the range 0..65535.
             The value of this attribute is copied to the bridge priority
             attribute of the associated MAC bridge configuration data managed
             entity.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBrgMaxAge
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the maximum age for an entry in the
             spanning tree listing. It indicates the maximum
             age in seconds for received protocol information before
             it is discarded.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBrgHelloTime
oids.description:
This attribute provides the time interval between hello packets.
             It is the time interval, in seconds, that a bridge advertises its
             presence while as a root or attempting to become a root.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBrgForwardDelay
oids.description:
This attribute gives the time that the
             bridge on the Ethernet card in the ONU retains a packet before
             forwarding it. (It indicates the value in seconds that
             the bridge uses for Forward Delay when the bridge acts as the
             root.)

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBrgUnknownMacDiscard
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the treatment of MAC frames with
             unknown destination addresses.
             enabled: frames with unknown destination addresses will be
                     discarded.
             disabled: frames with unknown destination addresses will be
                     forwarded to all allowed ports.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBrgMacLearningDepth
oids.description:
This attribute specifies the maximum number of UNI MAC addresses
             to be learned by the bridge.
             The default value 0 specifies that there is no
             administratively-imposed limit.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPBrgAgingTime
oids.description:
This attribute specifies the age of dynamic filtering entries in
             the bridge database, after which unrefreshed entries are discarded.
             In accordance with [IEEE 802.1D] clause 7.9.2 and [IEEE 802.1Q]
             clause 8.8.3, the range is 10..1 000 000 seconds, with a resolution
             of 1 second and a default of 300 seconds.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPWanIpConfTable
oids.description: The ONU profile WAN IP configuration table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPWanIpConfEntry
oids.description: The ONU profile WAN IP configuration entry.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPWanIpIndex
oids.description: The ID of WAN IP.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPWanIpMode
oids.description: IP address configuration mode.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPWanVlanPrf
oids.description: The name of table 'zxAnGponOPVlanPrfTable'.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPWanHostId
oids.description:
The ONU IP host ID. The value 0 indicates this attribute
             is not configured.
             The following IP host ID format is defined:             
             MSB : Most Significant Byte
             LSB : Least Significant Byte        
             |      MSB     |    Byte 2    |       Byte 1      |    LSB     |
             |--------------|-----------------------------------------------|
             |              |              |IP-type | reserved | host ID    |
             |     (8bits)  |  (8bits)     | (1bit) | (7bits)  | (8bits)    |
             |--------------------------------------------------------------|             
             IP-type: the IP type of host ID(1 bit)
             0 represents IPv4
             1 represents IPv6

oids.name: zxAnGponOPWanPppoeAuthMode
oids.description:
This attribute is related to 'zxAnGponOPWanIpMode', and
             it is valid only when 'zxAnGponOPWanIpMode' is PPPoE.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPWanPppoeUsername
oids.description:
This attribute is related to 'zxAnGponOPWanIpMode', and it is
             valid only when 'zxAnGponOPWanIpMode' is PPPoE.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPWanPppoePassword
oids.description:
This attribute is related to 'zxAnGponOPWanIpMode', and it is
             valid only when 'zxAnGponOPWanIpMode' is PPPoE.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPWanRspPing
oids.description: This attribute indicates whether respond to pings enabled.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPWanRspTraceRoute
oids.description:
This attribute indicates whether respond to trace route enabled.
             This attribute is only valid for IPv4 and this value is
             disabled(2) for IPv6.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPWanRsEnable
oids.description:
This attribute indicates whether respond to router 
             solicitation (RS) messages enabled. 
             This attribute is only valid for IPv6 and this value is
             disabled(2) for IPv4.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPWanOnLinkPrefix
oids.description:
It is the manually provisioned on-link prefix used for destination 
             IPv6 addresses of IPv6 host services. The attribute is structured 
             as follows: 
             Prefix length, number of leading bits in the prefix that are valid 
             (1 byte). 
             Prefix (16 bytes).
             This attribute is only valid for IPv6 and this value is 0x0 
             for IPv4.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPWanPppoeServiceName
oids.description: The PPPoE service name.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPWanIpConfRowStatus
oids.description:
The status of this conceptual row.

             To create a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to createAndGo(4).

             To delete a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to destroy(6).

             To query this object, always return 1.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipConfTable
oids.description: The ONU profile VoIP configuration table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipConfEntry
oids.description: The ONU profile VoIP configuration entry.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipSignalProtoUsed
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the type of VoIP signaling protocol used
             for ONU. Only one type of protocol is allowed. Valid values are:
             0x01 = None
             0x02 = SIP
             0x03 = H.248
             0x04 = MGCP
             0x05 = Selected by Non-OMCI.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipConfMethodUsed
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the ONU configuration method.
             0x01 the ONU default - do not configure.
             0x02 indicate use of OMCI for VoIP service configuration of
                  the ONU.
             0x03 indicate use of configuration file retrieval for VoIP service
                  configuration of the ONU.
             0x04 indicate use of TR-69 for VoIP service configuration of
                  the ONU.
             0x05 indicate use of IETF sipping config framework for VoIP
                  service configuration of the ONU.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipDomainName
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the ONU domain name that is used to locate 
             ONU by SS when the mode of MG registered to SS is using domain 
             name.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipH248EquipName
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the ONU equipment name that is used to 
             locate ONU by SS when the mode of MG registered to SS is using 
             equipment name.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipH248RegMode
oids.description: This attribute indicates the MID flag.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipMgcRegMode
oids.description: This attribute indicates the register mode.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipLineName
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the line name sequence number.
             The default value is the empty string.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpAllocMode
oids.description: This attribute indicates the VoIP allocate mode.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipSvrAddr
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the address of the server to contact
             using the method indicated in the VoIP configuration method used
             attribute. This attribute is only relevant for non-OMCI
             configuration methods. If this attribute is set to a null pointer,
             no address is defined by this attribute. However, the address may
             be defined by other methods, such as deriving it from the ONU
             identifier attribute of the IP host configuration data ME and using 
             a well-known URI schema.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipSvrValidation
oids.description: This attribute indicates the user validation scheme.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipSvrUsername
oids.description: This attribute indicates the user name for validation.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipSvrPassword
oids.description: This attribute indicates the password for validation.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipSvrRealm
oids.description:
This attribute indicates a string to be displayed to users so they 
             know which user name and password to use.  This string should 
             contain at least the name of the host performing the authentication 
             and might additionally indicate the collection of users who might 
             have access. An example might be 'registered_users@gotham. news
             .com'. The realm is a 'quoted-string' as specified in section 2.2 
             of the HTTP/1.1 specification.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipSvrRetrieveFile
oids.description:
This attribute indicates an action. It will be set independently
             to notify ONU to retrieve file again.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpConfTable
oids.description: The ONU profile VoIP IP configuration table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpConfEntry
oids.description: The ONU profile VoIP IP configuration entry.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpSlotId
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the index of slot in ONU.
             If slot ID is zero, it means the ONU is box-type,
             which can not plug card.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpMediaIndex
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the whether the separation of media
             and signaling. If 'media' is configured,The media will use a
             separation IP address, otherwise media and signaling will share
             the same IP address.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpMode
oids.description: This attribute indicates the IP address configuration mode.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpPrf
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the VoIP IP profile Name, it refers to 
             'zxAnGponRmIpPrfName'. If no profile name is indicated, this 
             attribute is set to a empty string.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpVlanPrf
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the VoIP IP VLAN profile Name, it refers 
             to 'zxAnGponRmVlanPrfName'.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpHostId
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the ONU VoIP host ID. 
             The value 0 indicates this attribute is not configured.
             The following VoIP host ID format is defined:             
             MSB : Most Significant Byte
             LSB : Least Significant Byte        
             |      MSB     |    Byte 2    |       Byte 1      |    LSB     |
             |--------------|-----------------------------------------------|
             |              |              |IP-type | reserved | host ID    |
             |     (8bits)  |  (8bits)     | (1bit) | (7bits)  | (8bits)    |
             |--------------------------------------------------------------|             
             IP-type: the IP type of host ID(1 bit)
             0 represents IPv4
             1 represents IPv6

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpAddrType
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the type of IP address denoted
             in zxAnGponOPVoipIpAddr object.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpAddr
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the VoIP IP address.
             This attribute is relation to 'zxAnGponOPVoipIpMode'.
             This attribute is valid only when 'zxAnGponOPVoipIpMode'
             is static.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpAddrPfxLen
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the VoIP IP address prefix length.
             This attribute is only valid for IPv4 and this value is 0
             for IPv6.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpPppoeMode
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the VoIP IP PPPoE mode.
             This attribute is relation to 'zxAnGponOPVoipIpMode'.
             This attribute is valid only when 'zxAnGponOPVoipIpMode' is PPPoE.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpPppoeUsername
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the VoIP IP PPPoE user name.
             This attribute is relation to 'zxAnGponOPVoipIpMode'.
             This attribute is valid only when 'zxAnGponOPVoipIpMode' is PPPoE.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpPppoePassword
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the VoIP IP PPPoE password.
             This attribute is relation to 'zxAnGponOPVoipIpMode'.
             This attribute is valid only when 'zxAnGponOPVoipIpMode' is PPPoE.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpRspPing
oids.description: This attribute indicates whether respond to pings enabled.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpRspTraceRoute
oids.description:
This attribute indicates whether respond to trace route enabled.
             This attribute is only valid for IPv4 and this value is
             disabled(2) for IPv6.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpRsEnable
oids.description:
This attribute indicates enabling or disabling response to router 
             solicitation (RS) messages function. This attribute is only valid
             for IPv6 and this value is disabled(2) for IPv4.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpOnLinkPrefix
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the manually provisioned on-link prefix 
             used for destination IPv6 addresses of IPv6 host services. 
             The attribute is structured as follows: 
             Prefix length, number of leading bits in the prefix that are valid 
             (1 byte)+ prefix (16 bytes).
             This attribute is only valid for IPv6 and this value is 0x0 
             for IPv4.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpPppoeServiceName
oids.description: This attribute indicates the PPPoE service name.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVoipIpConfRowStatus
oids.description:
The status of this conceptual row.

           The supported actions of this conceptual row only include:

           To create a row in this table, a manager must
           set this object to createAndGo(4).

           To delete a row in this table, a manager must
           set this object to destroy(6).

           To query this object, always return 1.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPUniDhcpIpRetrieveTable
oids.description:
The ONU profile Ethernet UNI's DHCP controlling configuration
             table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPUniDhcpIpRetrieveEntry
oids.description:
The ONU profile Ethernet UNI's DHCP controlling configuration
             entry.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPUniDhcpIpRetrieveMode
oids.description:
This attribute indicates the Ethernet UNI how to obtain its
             IP address (from ONU or network), when ONU enables the DHCP server
             function.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPIpStaticRouteTable
oids.description:
This table contains information of ONU IP static routes
             configuration. This managed entity stores IP static routes.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPIpStaticRouteEntry
oids.description:
It is entry in the zxAnGponOPIpStaticRouteTable. The index
            'zxAnGponOPName' represents the normal ONU index.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPIpRouteDestType
oids.description:
The type of the 'zxAnGponOPIpRouteDest' address, as defined
            in the InetAddress MIB.
            Only those address types that may appear in an actual
            routing table are allowed as values of this object.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPIpRouteDest
oids.description:
The destination IP address of this route.
             The type of this address is determined by the value of
             the 'zxAnGponOPIpRouteDestType' object.
             The values for the index objects 'zxAnGponOPIpRouteDest' and
             'zxAnGponOPIpRoutePfxLen' must be consistent.  When the value
             of 'zxAnGponOPIpRouteDest' (excluding the zone index, if one
             is present) is x, then the bitwise logical-AND of x with the
             value of the mask formed from the corresponding index object
             'zxAnGponOPIpRoutePfxLen' must be equal to x. If not, then the
             index pair is not consistent and an inconsistentName error must
             be returned on SET or CREATE requests.
             This field may be set to the default route address  0.0.0.0 .

oids.name: zxAnGponOPIpRoutePfxLen
oids.description:
Indicates the number of leading one bits that form the
             mask to be logical-ANDed with the destination address
             before being compared to the value in the
             zxAnGponOPIpRouteDest field.
             The values for the index objects 'zxAnGponOPIpRouteDest' and
             'zxAnGponOPIpRoutePfxLen' must be consistent.  When the value
             of 'zxAnGponOPIpRouteDest' (excluding the zone index, if one
             is present) is x, then the bitwise logical-AND of x with the
             value of the mask formed from the corresponding index object
             'zxAnGponOPIpRoutePfxLen' must be equal to x.  If not, then
             the index pair is not consistent and an inconsistentName error
             must be returned on SET or CREATE requests.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPIpRouteNextHopType
oids.description:
The type of the 'zxAnGponOPIpRouteNextHopType' address, as
            defined in the InetAddress MIB.
            Value should be set to unknown(0) for non-remote  routes.
            Only those address types that may appear in an actual
            routing table are allowed as values of this object.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPIpRouteNextHop
oids.description:
On remote routes, the address of the next system route.
             For non-remote routes, a zero length string.
             The type of this address is determined by the value of the
             'zxAnGponOPIpRouteNextHopType' object.
             This field is not used when the next hop is reached through an
             unnumbered link.
             For IPv4, the value is '255.255.255.255' when not used.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPIpRouteOutPort
oids.description:
The number of the IP port through which the next hop should be
             reached. This field is used when the next hop is reached through
             an unnumbered link and when a static route is set to support a
             fully meshed network. The value 0xFF indicates not used.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPIpRouteRowStatus
oids.description:
The status of this conceptual row.
             To create a row in this table, a manager must set this object to
             createAndGo(4). In particular, a newly created row cannot be
             created until the corresponding attributes from
             'zxAnGponOPIpRouteNextHopType' to 'zxAnGponOPIpRouteOutPort'
             have been set. To delete a row in this table, a manager must
             set this object to destroy(6).

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVeipTable
oids.description:
zxAnGponOPVeipTable table.
             This managed entity represents the data plane hand-off point in
             an ONU to a separate (non-OMCI) management domain.
             The virtual Ethernet interface point is managed by OMCI,
             and is potentially known to the non-OMCI management domain.
             One or more Ethernet traffic flows are present at this boundary.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVeipEntry
oids.description:
GPON VEIP configuration entry. The index
            'zxAnGponOPName' represents the normal ONU index or the virtual
             ONU index for profile.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVeipIndex
oids.description:
This attribute uniquely identifies each instance
             of this managed entity.
             To facilitate easy discovery,
             the ONU should assign IDs in the sequence 1, 2, ....
             The values 0 and 0xFFFF are reserved and not used now.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVeipAdminState
oids.description:
This attribute locks and unlocks the functions performed by
             this managed entity.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVeipInterdomainName
oids.description:
This attribute is a character string that provides an optional way
             to identify the virtual Ethernet interface point
             to a non-OMCI management domain.
             The interface may also be identified by its managed entity ID,
             IANA assigned port and possibly other ways.
             If the vendor offers no information in this attribute,
             it should be set to a sequence of null bytes.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVeipIsPortAssigned
oids.description:
This attribute points to an instance of the TCP/UDP config data
             managed entity, which provides for OMCI management of
             the non-OMCI management domain's IP connectivity.
             This attribute specifies whether to assign tcp or udp port
             for IP connectivity.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVeipProtoType
oids.description:
This attribute points to an instance of the TCP/UDP config data
             managed entity, which provides for OMCI management of
             the non-OMCI management domain's IP connectivity.
             This attribute specifies the protocol used in IP connectivity.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVeipAssignedPort
oids.description:
This attribute points to an instance of the TCP/UDP config data
             managed entity, which provides for OMCI management of
             the non-OMCI management domain's IP connectivity.
             This attribute specifies the assigned tcp or udp port
             for IP connectivity when zxAnGponOPVeipIsTcpOrUdpPortAssigned is
             true.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPVeipHostId
oids.description:
This attribute points to an instance of the TCP/UDP config data
             managed entity, which provides for OMCI management of
             the non-OMCI management domain's IP connectivity
             when zxAnGponOPVeipIsTcpOrUdpPortAssigned is true.
             This attribute specifies the host ID for IP connectivity.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTr069AcsTable
oids.description:
zxAnGponOPTr069AcsTable table.
             If functions within the ONU are managed by [BBF TR-69],
             this ME allows OMCI configuration of the ACS URL
             and related authentication information for an ACS connection
             initiated by the ONU.
             TR-69 supports other means to discover its ACS,
             so not all TR-69-compatible ONUs necessarily support this ME.
             Further, even if the ONU does support this ME,
             some operators may choose not to use it.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTr069AcsEntry
oids.description:
GPON Tr069 management server configuration entry. The index
            'zxAnGponOPName' represents the normal ONU index or the virtual
             ONU index for profile.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTr069AcsAdminState
oids.description:
This attribute locks and unlocks the functions performed
             by this managed entity. When the administrative state is locked,
             the functions of this ME are disabled.
             TR-69 connectivity to an ACS may be possible through means
             that do not depend on this ME.
             The default value of this attribute is locked.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTr069AcsUri
oids.description:
This attribute points to the large string ME that contains
             the network address.
             It may contain a fully qualified domain name,
             URI or IP address. The URI may also contain a port identifier,
             eg x.y.z.com:5060.
             A null pointer indicates that no network address is defined.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTr069AcsValidation
oids.description:
This attribute specifies the validation scheme used when the ONU
             validates a challenge. Validation schemes are defined as follows:
             1  Validation disabled.
             2  Validate using MD5 digest authentication as defined in
                [IETF RFC 2069].
             3  Validate using basic authentication as defined in
                [IETF RFC 2617].

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTr069AcsUsername
oids.description:
This string attribute is the user name. If the string is shorter
             than 25 bytes, it must be null terminated.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTr069AcsPassword
oids.description:
This string attribute is the password. If the string is shorter
             than 25 bytes, it must be null terminated.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTr069AcsRealm
oids.description:
This string attribute specifies the realm used in md5 digest
             authentication. If the string is shorter than 25 bytes,
             it must be null terminated.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPTr069AssociatedTag
oids.description:
This attribute is a TCI value for TR-69 management traffic
             passing through the virtual ethernet interface point.
             A TCI, comprising user priority, CFI and VID,
             is represented by 2 bytes.
             the first 3 bits represent priority.
             the last 12 bits represent VID.
             eg: priority(3 bits)| reserved(1 bits) |VID(12bits).
             The value 0xFFFF specifies that TR-69 management traffic
             passes through the virtual ethernet interface point
             with neither a VLAN nor a priority tag.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPEthUniTable
oids.description: The ONU profile Ethernet UNI configuration table.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPEthUniEntry
oids.description: The ONU profile Ethernet UNI configuration entry.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPEthUniExpectedType
oids.description:
The following coding is used for this attribute:
             0x00 = by autosensing, 0x01 to 0xFE (1 to 254) = one of the values
             from table 3 that is compatible with the type of the LIM. Upon
             autonomous instantiation, the value 0x00 is used.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPEthUniIfSpeedConf
zxAnGponOPEthUniLoopbackConf
oids.description:
This attribute is used to set the Ethernet loopback configuration:
             No loopback (value 0x01), loop3 (value 0x04, Loopback of downstream
             traffic after PHY transceiver).

oids.name: zxAnGponOPEthUniAdminState
oids.description:
This attribute is used to activate (unlock: value 0x01) and
             deactivate(lock: value 0x02) the functions performed by instances
             of this managed entity.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPEthUniMaxFrameSize
oids.description:
This attribute denotes the maximum allowed frame size to be
             transmitted across this interface.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPEthUniDteOrDceInd
oids.description:
This attribute specifies the Ethernet interface wiring:
             1 DCE or MDI-X (default).
             2 DTE or MDI.
             3 Automatic selection

oids.name: zxAnGponOPEthUniPauseTime
oids.description:
This attribute allows the ONU line card to request that the
             customer terminal temporarily suspend sending data. Units are in
             pause quanta(1 pause_quantum equals 512 bits times of the
             particular implementation).

oids.name: zxAnGponOPEthUniBridgeOrIpInd
oids.description:
This attribute specifies whether the Ethernet interface is bridged
             or derived from an IP router function.
             1 Bridged
             2 IP router
             3 Depends on the parent circuit pack.

             3 means that the circuit pack's bridged or IP ind attribute is
             either 1 or 2.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPEthUniArcEnable
oids.description:
This attribute allows the activation of alarm reporting control
             (ARC) for this PPTP or cardholder. The attribute works in concert
             with the ARC interval attribute.
             enabled: enables ARC.
             disabled: disables ARC.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPEthUniArcInterval
oids.description:
This attribute defines the interval to be used with the ARC
             function for this PPTP or cardholder. The values 0 through 254
             give the duration in minutes for the NALM-QI timer of M.3100.
             The special value 255 means that the timer never expires.
             The default value is zero.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPEthUniPppoeFilter
oids.description:
This attribute may be used to control filtering of PPPoE packets
             on this Ethernet port. The value 2 is used to disable filtering.
             The value 1 is used to enable filtering. When filtering is
             enabled, all packets other than PPPoE packets will be discarded.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPEthUniPowerFeed
oids.description:
This attribute controls whether power is provided to an external
             equipment over the Ethernet PPTP. Value 1 enables power over
             Ethernet port. The default value 2 disables power feed.

oids.name: zxAnGponOPEthUniLoopDetect
oids.description: This attribute enable or disable the loopback detection.

oids.name: zxAnPonRxOpticalPowerTable
oids.description: Received optical power on OLT side from ONU.

oids.name: zxAnPonRxOpticalPowerEntry
oids.description:
Entry of zxAnPonRxOpticalPowerTable.
            The index 'zxAnPonOnuIndex' represents the normal ONU index.

oids.name: zxAnPonRxOpticalPower
oids.description:
Measured the received optical power. 
            65535000 means N/A, ONU is not in service.
            -80000 means no signal received.

oids.name: zxAnPonRxOpticalPowerThreshTable
oids.description: Received threshold of transceiver on OLT side.

oids.name: zxAnPonRxOpticalPowerThreshEntry
oids.description:
Entry of zxAnPonRxOpticalPowerThreshTable.
            The index 'zxAnPonOnuIndex' represents the normal ONU index.

oids.name: zxAnPonRxPowerRisingAlarmThresh
oids.description: Rising alarm threshold of received power.

oids.name: zxAnPonRxPowerFallingAlarmThresh
oids.description: Falling alarm threshold of received power.

oids.name: zxAnPonRxPowerDetectPeriod
oids.description: Detect period.

oids.name: zxAnPonRxPowerRestoreDefault
oids.description:
The action of the ONU, when do the action, the 
             'zxAnPonRxPowerRisingAlarmThresh' value and 
             'zxAnPonRxPowerFallingAlarmThresh' value will be restored to the 
             system defaule value.

oids.name: zxAnPonGisLongitude
oids.description: The longitude of the PON equipment.

oids.name: zxAnPonGisLatitude
oids.description: The latitude of the PON equipment.

oids.name: zxAnPonGisElevation
oids.description: The elevation of the PON equipment.

oids.name: zxAnPonGisAltitudeError
oids.description:
The altitude error of the PON equipment. It caused by the changing 
             of altitude location during the measurement.

oids.name: zxAnPonGisAreaCode
oids.description:
The GIS area code of the PON equipment. 
             It references from the latest version 2013 of 
             NBS(National Bureau of Statistics of China),
             http://www.stats.gov.cn/tjsj/tjbz/tjyqhdmhcxhfdm/2013/index.html.

oids.name: zxAnPonGisTimestamp
oids.description:
The timestamp of GIS generated by BUAPP.
             The offset time based on 0 hour 0 minute 0 second Jan 1st 1970 
             in seconds.

oids.name: zxAnPonGisDigest
oids.description: The GIS digest.

oids.name: zxAnPonGisCheckCode
oids.description:
The GIS check code. In order to prevent user input wrong 
             information, the OLT will generate a new check code according to 
             the calculation of above 8 variables, and compare the new check 
             code with the check code generated by BUAPP.
